explanation:
We have,

$$\begin{aligned} & I=\int \frac{d x}{\cos 2 x-\cos ^2 x} \\ & I=\int \frac{d x}{2 \cos ^2 x-1-\cos ^2 x} \\ & I=\int \frac{d x}{-\sin ^2 x} \\ & I=-\int \operatorname{cosec}^2 x d x \\ & I=\cot x+c \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $$x+\log \left(1-2 e^{-x}\right)+c$$
$$x+2 \log \left(1-2 e^{-x}\right)+c$$
$$\log \left(1-2 e^{-x}\right)+c$$
$$x-\log \left(1-2 e^{-x}\right)+c$$

explanation:
We have,

$$\begin{aligned} & I=\int \frac{1+2 \theta^{-x}}{1-2 \theta^{-x}} d x \\ & I=\int\left(\frac{1-2 \theta^{-x}}{1-2 \theta^{-x}}\right) d x+\int \frac{4 \theta^{-x}}{1-2 \theta^{-x}} d x \\ & I=\int d x+2 \int \frac{d t}{t} \\ &\left.\quad \quad [ \because \text { put } 1-2 \theta^{-x}=t \Rightarrow 2 e^{-x} d x=d t\right] \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=x+2 \log t+c \\ & \Rightarrow \quad I=x+2 \log \left|1-2 \theta^{-x}\right|+c \end{aligned}$$

We have,

$$\begin{aligned} & I=\int \frac{\sin \theta}{\sin 3 \theta} d \theta \\ & I=\int \frac{\sin \theta}{3 \sin \theta-4 \sin ^3 \theta} d \theta \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & I=\int \frac{d \theta}{3-4 \sin ^2 \theta} \\ & I=\int \frac{\sec ^2 \theta d \theta}{3 \sec ^2 \theta-4 \tan ^2 \theta}\left[\because \text { divide by } \cos ^2 \theta\right] \\ & I=\int \frac{\sec ^2 \theta d \theta}{3+3 \tan ^2 \theta-4 \tan ^2 \theta} \\ & I=\int \frac{\sec ^2 \theta d \theta}{3-\tan ^2 \theta} \end{aligned}$$

Put, $$\tan \theta=t \Rightarrow \sec ^2 \theta d \theta=d t$$

$$\begin{aligned} \quad I & =\int \frac{d t}{(\sqrt{3})^2-t^2}=\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{3}} \log \left|\frac{\sqrt{3}+t}{\sqrt{3}-t}\right|+C \\ \quad I & =\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{3}} \log \left|\frac{\sqrt{3}+\tan \theta}{\sqrt{3}-\tan \theta}\right|+C \\ \therefore \quad K & =\sqrt{3} \end{aligned}$$

We have,

$$\begin{aligned} & I=\int \sqrt{x-\frac{1}{x}}\left(\frac{x^2+1}{x^2}\right) d x \\ & I=\int \sqrt{x-\frac{1}{x}}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x \end{aligned}$$

Put, $$\quad x-\frac{1}{x}=t \Rightarrow\left(1+\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x=d t$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \quad I=\int \sqrt{t} ~d t \\ & \quad I=\frac{2}{3}(t)^{3 / 2}+C \\ & \quad I=\frac{2}{3}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^{3 / 2}+C \\ & \therefore \quad K=3 / 2 \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $$\log (\sin x)[\log (\log (\sin x))-1]+c$$
$$\log (\sin x)[\log (\log (\sin x))+1]+c$$
$$\log (\sin x)[\log (\sin x))+1]+c$$
$$\log (\sin x)[\log (\sin x)-1]+c$$

explanation:
We have,

$$I=\int \cot x \cdot \log (\log \sin x) d x$$

Put, log sin $$x=t \Rightarrow \cot x d x=d t$$

$$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad I & =\int \log t d t \\ \quad I & =\log t \int d t-\int\left(\frac{d \log t}{d t}\right) \cdot \int d t \cdot d t+C \\ \quad I & =t \log t-\int \frac{1}{t} \times t d t+C \\ & I=t \log t-\int d t+C \\ & I=t \log t-t+C \\ \therefore \quad \quad & I=t(\log t-1)+C \\ & I=\log (\sin x)[\log (\log (\sin x))-1]+C \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $\frac{x}{1+\log x}+c$
$x(1-\log x)+c$

explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} \text{Let }\quad I & =\int \log x[\log (e x)]^{-2} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\log x}{(\log e x)^2} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\log x}{(\log e+\log x)^2} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\log x}{(1+\log x)^2} d x \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} \text { Put } & & \log x & =t \Rightarrow x=e^t \\ \Rightarrow & & d x & =e^t d t \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\int \frac{t}{(1+t)^2} e^t d t \\ & =\int e^t\left(\frac{1}{1+t}-\frac{1}{(1+t)^2}\right) d t \\ & =\frac{e^t}{1+t}+C \\ & =\frac{x}{(1+\log x)}+C \end{aligned}$$

content: If $\int \frac{1}{1-\cot x} d x=A x+B \log |\sin x-\cos x|+c$ then $A+B=\ldots \ldots$
explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} \text { Let } I& =\int \frac{1}{1-\cot x} d x \\ & =\int \frac{1}{1-\frac{\cos x}{\sin x}} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\sin x}{\sin x-\cos x} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{2 \sin x}{\sin x-\cos x} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{\sin x+\sin x+\cos x-\cos x}{\sin x-\cos x} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \int \frac{(\sin x-\cos x)+(\sin x+\cos x)}{\sin x-\cos x} d x \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \left[\int d x+\int \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{\sin x-\cos x} d x\right. \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } \sin x-\cos x=t \\ & \Rightarrow(\cos x+\sin x) d x=d t \\ & \therefore \quad I=\frac{1}{2}\left[\int d x+\int \frac{1}{t} d t\right] \\ & =\frac{1}{2}[x+\log |t|]+C \\ & =\frac{1}{2}[x+\log |\sin x-\cos x|]+C \\ & =\frac{x}{2}+\frac{1}{2} \log |\sin x-\cos x|+C \\ & \text { Here, } A=\frac{1}{2}, B=\frac{1}{2} \\ & \therefore A+B=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}=1 \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $\log |\sin x+\cos x|+c$

$\log \left|\frac{\sin x+\cos x}{2 \cos x-\sin x}\right|+c$
$\log \left|\frac{\tan x+1}{\tan x+2}\right|+c$

explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} \text { Let } I & =\int \frac{d x}{(\sin x+\cos x)(2 \cos x+\sin x)} \\ & =\int \frac{\sec ^2 x}{(\tan x+1)(2+\tan x)} d x \end{aligned}$$

Put $\tan x=t$

$$\Rightarrow \quad \sec ^2 x d x=d t \int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}$$

Here,$\frac{1}{(t+1)(t+2)}=\frac{A}{t+1}+\frac{B}{t+2}$

$$\begin{aligned} &\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad 1=A(t+2)+B(t+1) \\ & \text { Put } \quad t=-2 \\ & 1=B(-1) \Rightarrow B=-1 \\ & \therefore \text { Put } t+1=0 \Rightarrow t=-1 \\ & \therefore \quad 1=A(1) \Rightarrow A=1 \end{aligned}\\ &\text { Now, } \int \frac{d t}{(t+1)(t+2)}=\int \frac{1}{t+1} d t-\int \frac{1}{t+2} d t \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\log (t+1)-\log (t+2)+ C \\ & =\log \left(\frac{t+1}{t+2}\right)+C \\ & =\log \left(\frac{\tan x+1}{\tan x+2}\right)+C \end{aligned}$$

content: If $$\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{8-\sin 2 x} d x=\frac{1}{p} \log \left[\frac{3+\sin x+\cos x}{3-\sin x-\cos x}\right]+c,$$ then $p=$ .............
explanation:
We have,

$$\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{8-\sin 2 x}=\frac{1}{p} \log \left[\frac{3+\sin x+\cos x}{3-\sin x-\cos x}\right]+C$$

Now, $\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{8-\sin 2 x} d x$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{9-(1+2 \sin x \cos x)} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{9-\left(\sin ^2 x+\cos ^2 x+2 \sin x \cos x\right)} d x \\ & =\int \frac{\cos x-\sin x}{(3)^2-(\cos x+\sin x)^2} d x \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { put } \cos x+\sin x=t \\ & \begin{aligned} &(-\sin x+\cos x) d x=d t \\ &=\int \frac{d t}{(3)^2-(t)^2}=\frac{1}{2(3)} \log \left|\frac{3+t}{3-t}\right|+C \\ & \quad= \frac{1}{6} \log \left|\frac{3+\sin x+\cos x}{3-\sin x-\cos x}\right|+C \\ & \therefore p=6 \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$

content: $$\begin{aligned} & \text { If } \int \tan (x-\alpha) \tan (x+\alpha) \cdot \tan 2 x d x \\ & =p \log |\sec 2 x|+q \log |\sec (x+\alpha)| \\ & +r \log |\sec (x-\alpha)|+c \text { then } p+q+r=\ldots \ldots \ldots \end{aligned}$$
explanation:
We have,

$$\begin{aligned} & \int \tan (x-\alpha) \tan (x+\alpha) \cdot \tan 2 x d x \\ & =p \log |\sec 2 x|+q \log |\sec (x+a)| \\ & \quad+r \log |\sec (x-a)|+C \end{aligned}$$

Here, $\tan 2 x=\tan ((x-\alpha)+(x+\alpha))$

$$\begin{aligned} & =\tan (x-\alpha)+\tan (x+\alpha) \\ & 1-\tan (x-\alpha) \tan (x+\alpha) \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \tan 2 x-\tan 2 x \tan (x-\alpha) \tan (x+\alpha) \\ & =\tan (x-\alpha)+\tan (x+\alpha) \\ & \Rightarrow \tan (x-\alpha) \tan (x+\alpha) \tan 2 x \\ & \Rightarrow \tan 2 x-\tan (x-\alpha)-\tan (x+\alpha) \\ & \therefore \int \tan (x-\alpha) \tan (x+\alpha) \tan (2 x) d x \\ & =\int(\tan 2 x-\tan (x-\alpha)-\tan (x+\alpha)) d x \\ & \left.=\frac{\log |\sec 2 x|}{2}-\log |\sec (x-\alpha)|-\log \right\rvert\, \sec (x+\alpha)+C \\ & =\frac{1}{2} \log |\sec 2 x|+(-1) \log |\sec (x+\alpha)| \\ & +(-1) \log \mid \sec (x-\alpha)+C \\ & \therefore p=\frac{1}{2}, g=-1 \text { and } r=-1 \\ & \therefore p+q+r=\frac{1}{2}+(-1)+(-1)=\frac{1}{2}-2=\frac{-3}{2} \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2+1}{2}\right)+c$

explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } I=\int \frac{x^2+1}{x^4-x^2+1} d x \\ & =\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{x^2}}{x^2-1+\frac{1}{x^2}} d x=\int \frac{1+\frac{1}{x^2}}{\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2+1} \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { put } x-\frac{1}{x}=t \\ & \qquad\left(1+\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x=d t \end{aligned}$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \therefore I=\int \frac{d t}{t^2+1}=\tan ^{-1}(t)+c \\ & =\tan ^{-1}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)+c=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x^2-1}{x}\right)+c \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $\log \left|\frac{x \sin x}{x+\cos x}\right|+c$
$\log \left|\frac{x}{x+\cos x}\right|+c$

explanation: $$\begin{aligned} & \int \frac{\cos x+x \sin x}{x^2+x \cos x} d x \\ &=\int \frac{\cos x+x \sin x+x-x}{x(x+\cos x)} d x \\ &=\int \frac{(\cos x+x)+x(\sin x-1)}{x(x+\cos x)} d x \\ &=\int \frac{1}{x} d x+\int \frac{\sin x-1}{x+\cos x} d x \\ &=\log |x|-\int \frac{1-\sin x}{x+\cos x} d x \\ &=\log |x|-\log (x+\cos x)+c \\ &=\log \left|\frac{x}{x+\cos x}\right|+c \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $\tan ^{-1} x-\frac{1}{2 x\left(x^2+1\right)}+c$

explanation:
$$\begin{aligned} & \text { } \int \frac{1}{x^2+1} d x=\frac{1}{x^2+1} \\ & \int 1 d x-\int\left(\frac{d}{d x}\left(\frac{1}{x^2+1}\right) \int 1 d x\right) d x \quad\text{(Integration by parts)}\\ & =\frac{x}{x^2+1}-\int \frac{-2 x}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2} x d x \\ & =\frac{x}{x^2+1}+2 \int \frac{x^2+1-1}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2} d x \\ & =\frac{x}{x^2+1}+2 \int \frac{x}{\left(x^2+1\right)} d x-2 \int \frac{d x}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2}\quad \text{.... (i)} \end{aligned}$$

And also, we know that

$$\int \frac{d x}{x^2+1}=\tan ^{-1}(x)\quad \text{.... (ii)}$$

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

$$\begin{aligned} & \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{x}{x^2+1}+2 \tan ^{-1} x-2 \int \frac{d x}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & 2 \int \frac{d x}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2}=\tan ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{x^2+1} \\ \Rightarrow \quad& \int \frac{d x}{\left(x^2+1\right)^2}=\frac{1}{2} \tan ^{-1} x+\frac{x}{2\left(x^2+1\right)}+c \end{aligned}$$

options.content: $\sqrt{x^2-a^2}-a \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{a}{x}\right)+c$
$x \sqrt{x^2-a^2}-\frac{1}{a} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+c$
$\sqrt{x^2-a^2}+a \sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)+c$

explanation:
Let $$I=\int \frac{\sqrt{x^2-a^2}}{x} d x$$

$$\begin{aligned} & \text { Putting } \quad x=a \sec \theta \Rightarrow \theta=\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right) \\ & \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow \quad d x=a \sec \theta \tan \theta d \theta \\ & \begin{aligned} \therefore \quad I & =\int \frac{\sqrt{a \sec ^2 \theta-a^2}}{a \sec \theta}(a \sec \theta \tan \theta d \theta) \\ & =\int a \tan \theta \cdot \tan \theta d \theta=\int a \tan ^2 \theta d \theta \\ & =a \int\left(\sec ^2 \theta-1\right) d \theta=a[\tan \theta-\theta]+C \\ & =a\left[\sqrt{\sec ^2 \theta-1}-\theta\right]+C \\ & =a\left[\sqrt{\frac{x^2}{a^2}-1}-\sec ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{a}\right)\right]+C \end{aligned} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad \sqrt{x^2-a^2}-a \cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{a}{x}\right)+C \end{aligned} \end{aligned}$$
